quarantine.reason: Ore-ore sagi — multi-day social engineering, Day 7 money request

signals.label: Behavioral Velocity
signals.desc: Trust-building over 7 days before financial ask

signals.label: Story Contradiction
signals.desc: Phone number does not match known grandson contact

quarantine.evidence_summary: Corpus match: 87% similarity to known ore-ore sagi scripts. Sender claimed relationship "grandson" but contact graph shows zero connections to any known contact. Behavioral velocity score 0.94 — anomalous trust-building cadence across 7 messages.
quarantine.reasoning: This sender followed the classic ore-ore pattern: establish familiarity (Day 1-3), build emotional bond (Day 4-5), introduce urgency (Day 6), request money (Day 7). The behavioral velocity alone flags this at 0.75 by Day 4. Combined with zero network connections and corpus match, confidence is near-certain.

quarantine.reason: Early warning — behavioral velocity anomaly, no scam signal yet

signals.label: Behavioral Velocity
signals.desc: Accelerating contact frequency from unknown sender

signals.label: Emotional Escalation
signals.desc: Increasingly personal tone without established relationship

quarantine.evidence_summary: No explicit scam signals detected. Flagged on behavioral pattern alone: 4 messages in 4 days from an unverified sender with escalating emotional tone. Velocity score 0.68 — above threshold for early warning.
quarantine.reasoning: The behavioral analyzer cannot yet classify this as scam — there are no financial asks, urgency markers, or identity contradictions. However, the velocity pattern is consistent with the grooming phase of ore-ore sagi. Flagging for family review to maintain human oversight during the ambiguous window.

quarantine.reason: Outbound interceptor — compliance reply to high-risk sender

signals.label: Outbound to Flagged Sender
signals.desc: Replying to sender with risk score > 0.75

signals.label: Financial Information
signals.desc: Message contains bank account reference pattern

signals.label: Compliance Language
signals.desc: Agreeing to action requested by flagged sender

quarantine.evidence_summary: Protected user attempted to reply to けんじ (risk 0.98). Outbound message matched financial information pattern (bank account format detected). Compliance language detected: affirmative response to previous request.
quarantine.reasoning: The outbound interceptor held this message because the recipient has a near-certain scam classification. The protected user appears to be complying with the scammer's request. This is the critical interception point — the scam would succeed if this message is delivered.

quarantine.reason: Impersonation of National Police Agency — authority fraud pattern

signals.label: Authority Impersonation
signals.desc: Claims to be 警察庁 (National Police Agency)

signals.label: Legal Threat
signals.desc: Threatens arrest if action not taken

quarantine.evidence_summary: Sender domain does not match any legitimate police communication channel. Message template matches known authority impersonation corpus (92% similarity). No legitimate police contact exists in protected user's history.
quarantine.reasoning: Authority impersonation scams targeting elderly Japanese citizens commonly use police, tax office, or bank identities. This message uses all three canonical markers: false authority, legal threat, and financial demand. Blocked immediately — no ambiguity.

quarantine.reason: Appointment reminder from known clinic — auto-released after review

signals.label: Known Contact
signals.desc: Sender in trusted contact list since 2024-03

quarantine.evidence_summary: Sender matches known medical provider in contact graph. Message pattern consistent with previous appointment reminders. No anomalous signals detected.
quarantine.reasoning: Briefly flagged by the velocity filter due to an unusual send time (late evening). Automatic review confirmed the sender identity against the contact graph. Auto-released within 30 seconds — no human intervention needed.

quarantine.reason: Elder abuse signal — financial control pattern from known contact

signals.label: Financial Control
signals.desc: Repeated requests for account access from close contact

signals.label: Isolation Language
signals.desc: Discouraging contact with other family members

quarantine.evidence_summary: 田中太郎 is a known contact (friend) but recent messages show a pattern shift: 3 requests for financial information in 2 weeks, combined with language discouraging the protected user from discussing finances with family. Pattern matches EA-1 (financial control) criteria.
quarantine.reasoning: Elder abuse detection operates on a different axis than scam detection. This sender is verified and trusted, but the behavioral pattern has shifted toward financial control. The risk is lower than external scams but the signal warrants family review. Flagging without blocking to preserve the relationship while alerting the family guardian.
